description: Vectorized NumPy array access for wall attributes.

functions.name: cutoff
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the wall cutoff angle.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.wallarray.cutoff()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

functions.name: disp
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the wall displacement.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.wallarray.disp()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

functions.name: euler
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the wall orientation.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.wallarray.euler()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
This function is version-specific within the supported PFC 6.0/7.0/9.0 documentation set.

functions.name: extra
functions.signature: itasca.wallarray.extra (slot : int) -> array float{wall} or float{wall,3}.
functions.description: Get the wall extra data in the given slot as an array. Extra variables accessed by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.wallarray.extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: fill_cutoff
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the wall cutoff angle. The array must be the correct shape.

examples.code:
import numpy as np
import itasca as it
data = np.empty_like(it.wallarray.cutoff())
it.wallarray.fill_cutoff(data)
examples.description: Fill a preallocated NumPy array to avoid allocating a new result array.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
The destination array must already have the correct shape and dtype.

functions.name: set_cutoff
functions.description: Set the wall cutoff angle from an array.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.wallarray.set_cutoff()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_extra
functions.signature: itasca.wallarray.set_extra (slot: int, data: array float{wall} or float{wall, 3}) -> None.
functions.description: Set the wall extra data in the given slot with an array. Extra variables set by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.wallarray.set_extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_group
functions.signature: itasca.wallarray.set_group (membership: array bool{wall}, group_name: str, slot=1) -> None.
functions.description: Set wall group from an array. Where membership True set the corresponding wall to be a member of group group_name in the given slot.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.wallarray.set_group()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.

functions.name: set_pos
functions.description: Set the wall location from an array.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
positions = it.wallarray.pos()
positions[:, 2] += 0.1
it.wallarray.set_pos(positions)
examples.description: Modify positions in bulk and write them back.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
